en: I want you to know, brothers, that our fathers were all under the cloud, and all passed through the sea,
zh: 弟兄們，我不願意你們不知道，我們的祖宗都曾經在雲下，都曾經從海中經過，

en: and all were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea,
zh: 都曾經在雲裡在海裡受洗歸於摩西。

en: and all ate the same spiritual food,
zh: 他們都吃了一樣的靈糧，

en: and all drank the same spiritual drink. For they drank from the spiritual Rock that followed them, and the Rock was Christ.
zh: 都喝了一樣的靈水；他們所喝的，是從那隨著他們的靈磐石那裡來的，這磐石就是基督。

en: Nevertheless, with most of them God was not pleased, for they were overthrown in the wilderness.
zh: 但他們大多數的人，都得不到 神的喜悅，因此他們都死在曠野。

en: Now these things took place as examples for us, that we might not desire evil as they did.
zh: 這些事都是我們的鑒戒，叫我們不要貪戀惡事，像他們那樣。

en: Do not be idolaters as some of them were; as it is written, "The people sat down to eat and drink and rose up to play."
zh: 你們也不可拜偶像，像他們有些人那樣；正如經上所記：“人民坐下吃喝，起來玩樂。”

en: We must not indulge in sexual immorality as some of them did, and twenty-three thousand fell in a single day.
zh: 我們也不可淫亂，像他們有些人那樣，一天就死了二萬三千人。

en: We must not put Christ to the test, as some of them did and were destroyed by serpents,
zh: 我們也不可試探主，像他們有些人那樣，結果就被蛇咬死了。

en: nor grumble, as some of them did and were destroyed by the Destroyer.
zh: 你們也不可發怨言，像他們有些人那樣，就被那毀滅者所滅。

en: Now these things happened to them as an example, but they were written down for our instruction, on whom the end of the ages has come.
zh: 這些事發生在他們身上，作為鑒戒，並且記下來，為了要警戒我們這些末世的人。

en: Therefore let anyone who thinks that he stands take heed lest he fall.
zh: 所以，那自以為站得穩的，應當謹慎，免得跌倒。

en: No temptation has overtaken you that is not common to man. God is faithful, and he will not let you be tempted beyond your ability, but with the temptation he will also provide the way of escape, that you may be able to endure it.
zh: 你們所受的試探，無非是人受得起的； 神是信實的，他必不容許你們受試探過於你們承受得起的，而且在受試探的時候，必定給你們開一條出路，使你們能忍受得住。

en: Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.
zh: 所以，我所親愛的，你們要遠避拜偶像的事。

en: I speak as to sensible people; judge for yourselves what I say.
zh: 我是對明白事理的人說的，我所說的你們要自己判斷。

en: The cup of blessing that we bless, is it not a participation in the blood of Christ? The bread that we break, is it not a participation in the body of Christ?
zh: 我們為福杯祝禱的時候，難道不是共享基督的血嗎？我們擘餅的時候，難道不是共享基督的身體嗎？

en: Because there is one bread, we who are many are one body, for we all partake of the one bread.
zh: 因為事實上只有一個餅，我們人數雖多，還是一個身體，因為我們都是分享同一個餅。

en: Consider the people of Israel: are not those who eat the sacrifices participants in the altar?
zh: 你們看看按肉身是以色列人，那些吃祭物的不就是與祭壇有分嗎？

en: What do I imply then? That food offered to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything?
zh: 我說的是甚麼意思呢？偶像算得甚麼，還是祭過偶像的食物算得甚麼？

en: No, I imply that what pagans sacrifice they offer to demons and not to God. I do not want you to be participants with demons.
zh: 我是說，教外人所祭的是鬼，不是獻給 神；我卻不願意你們與鬼來往。

en: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You cannot partake of the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
zh: 你們不能喝主的杯又喝鬼的杯，你們不能參加主的筵席又參加鬼的筵席。

en: Shall we provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than he?
zh: 難道我們要激起主的忿怒嗎？我們比他更強嗎？

en: "All things are lawful," but not all things are helpful. "All things are lawful," but not all things build up.
zh: 甚麼事情都可以作，但不是都有益處。甚麼事情都可以作，但不是都能造就人。

en: Let no one seek his own good, but the good of his neighbor.
zh: 人不要求自己的好處，卻要求別人的好處。

en: Eat whatever is sold in the meat market without raising any question on the ground of conscience.
zh: 肉食市場所賣的一切，你們只管吃，不要為了良心的緣故問甚麼，

en: For "the earth is the Lord's, and the fullness thereof."
zh: 因為地和地上所充滿的都是屬於主的。

en: If one of the unbelievers invites you to dinner and you are disposed to go, eat whatever is set before you without raising any question on the ground of conscience.
zh: 如果有不信的人邀請你們吃飯，你們也願意去，凡擺在你們面前的，都可以吃，不要為了良心的緣故問甚麼。

en: But if someone says to you, "This has been offered in sacrifice," then do not eat it, for the sake of the one who informed you, and for the sake of conscience-
zh: 但是如果有人告訴你們：“這是獻過祭的食物。”那麼，為了那告訴你們的人，也為了良心的緣故，就不要吃。

en: I do not mean your conscience, but his. For why should my liberty be determined by someone else's conscience?
zh: 我說的良心，不是你的，而是他的。為甚麼我的自由要受別人的良心論斷呢？

en: If I partake with thankfulness, why am I denounced because of that for which I give thanks?
zh: 我若存著感恩的心吃了，為甚麼我因著我所感恩的食物被人毀謗呢？

en: So, whether you eat or drink, or whatever you do, do all to the glory of God.
zh: 所以，你們或吃喝，或作甚麼，一切都要為 神的榮耀而行。

en: Give no offense to Jews or to Greeks or to the church of God,
zh: 無論是猶太人，是希臘人，是 神的教會，你們總不可使他們跌倒；

en: just as I try to please everyone in everything I do, not seeking my own advantage, but that of many, that they may be saved.
zh: 就好像我所作的一切，都是要使大家喜悅，不是要求自己的好處，而是要求別人的好處，為了要使他們得救。
